# Questions in Livestream 220

Live Stream #220: Bears, Bears, Everywheres

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:00</a> | What about the inscription Michael Barron thinks comes from Moses, even though it lacks a he and dates to the 18th century BC? | He says the channel will cover it in detail over the next two weeks and is already very critical of Barron's research. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=1341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:21</a> | Can information in the Liber Pontificalis be used, or should it be distrusted? | He treats it cautiously at best, arguing that obvious forgeries in the work make it unreliable as a straightforward source. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=1812" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:12</a> | Why should font choice matter in writing and literature? | Typography affects readability, tone, and the emotional force of the page. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=1977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:57</a> | Should morality always be placed above practicality when societies mandate solutions? | Not always; he argues governments sometimes tolerate lesser evils for practical stability. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=2173" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:13</a> | Does cultural homogenization lead to the death of individuality? | Only if it becomes extreme and totalizing; ordinary homogenization does not erase individuality by itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=2335" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:55</a> | What work of fiction best captures despair? | He does not offer a firm pick and says he does not usually read books by looking for that theme. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=2470" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:10</a> | What does 1 Peter 5:8 mean, especially the devil seeking to devour someone? | He reads it as a warning about spiritual destruction and the loss of faith, not literal chewing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=2772" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:12</a> | Is there any reason to get a DMin? | He says its main practical use for a pastor is the title and potential pay increase; it is not a research doctorate. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=2846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:26</a> | Could someone teach at a university with a DMin? | No; he says it is not a research doctorate and is not equivalent to a PhD for that role. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=3000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:00</a> | What are some tips for studying the prophets? | Start by learning biblical poetry, since prophetic books often speak poetically. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=3032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:32</a> | Is a DMin any better than an honorary doctorate? | Somewhat; he treats a DMin as at least a professional doctorate, while an honorary doctorate does not really let someone call themselves doctor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=3164" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:44</a> | How should someone answer the claim that saving faith in Jesus means keeping Old Testament law as before? | He calls that Judaizing and argues the old temple-centered law cannot be kept in that way and is fulfilled in Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=3340" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:40</a> | Are any other Robert Alter books worth a look? | He only strongly recommends The Art of Biblical Poetry; he says Alter's work on biblical prose or narrative is okay but not as strong. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=4602" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:42</a> | Would it make more sense to sell your books directly from your own website instead of through Amazon? | He says no for their situation because Canadian shipping costs make direct fulfillment unattractive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=4829" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:29</a> | What do you think of the claim that scholars found Moses's signature? | He is strongly skeptical and treats it as a fringe claim rather than a solid scholarly finding. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=5849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:29</a> | Have Western Christians tried to suppress or subvert Eastern Christianity? | He frames it more as neglect and ignorance than as a simple organized suppression campaign. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=6659" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:59</a> | Could the Tower of Babel story refer to the collapse of the Akkadian Empire and a slow linguistic drift? | He says that is possible in broad outline but too uncertain to pin down confidently to that period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=6743" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:23</a> | Is there any connection between Babel and Babylon? | Only in the shared idea of bab as "gate"; he says that does not prove the Babel story is set in Babylon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=6833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:53</a> | Is there an etymological connection between babbling and the Tower of Babel? | Yes through the English translation tradition around Babel and confused language, not because the Hebrew word directly means confusion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=6927" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:27</a> | Did Joseph actually use a divining cup in Genesis 44:5? | Yes; he thinks Joseph used one, as other high officials in that setting did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=7222" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:22</a> | Was Joseph's practice of divination illicit or sinful? | No, not at that point; he places it under a pre-Sinai patriarchal exemption. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=7449" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:09</a> | Could Pharaoh's obstinance be explained because the plagues resembled natural events seen before in Egypt? | Partly yes; familiar flood-related phenomena may have made them easier to rationalize, even though their scale was abnormal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=7717" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:37</a> | Did Moses actually want Pharaoh to let Israel go for just a few days, or was it a way to make Pharaoh angry? | He says the request had Egyptian parallels and was not merely provocation, but it exposed Pharaoh's distrust and refusal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=8013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:33</a> | Is Mary's perpetual virginity required, and why? | He says it is required only in churches that later dogmatized it, not across all Christian traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=8224" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:04</a> | Is it heretical to reject Theotokos when the term is properly defined? | Yes; he says rejecting it denies what Christians mean about Christ's full personhood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=8312" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:32</a> | Is Mary giving birth only to the flesh of Jesus? | No; he insists she gives birth to the full person of Christ, who is fully God and fully man. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=8590" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:10</a> | What are your general thoughts on Karl Barth? | He is critical of Barth's neo-orthodoxy and some aspects of Barth's personal life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=8902" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:28:22</a> | Do you know about Redeemed Zoomer, and can denominations be discussed lovingly and unitedly? | He has heard the name but does not watch much of him; he agrees denominations can be discussed respectfully and constructively. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=9072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:12</a> | Why do so many problematic theologians seem to come from Germany? | He ties that partly to the structure of German education and the philosophical currents that shaped it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=9341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:41</a> | What does the second part of Matthew 10:23 mean? | He reads it as Jesus saying the disciples will not finish that Israel-focused mission before the Son of Man comes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=9355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:55</a> | Have you gotten out of the shadowban yet? | No; he says YouTube shut off creator email notifications, which especially hurts smaller channels that depend on community engagement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=9896" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:56</a> | Could Matthew 10:23 and Matthew 16:27-28 refer to AD 70? | He does not think so; he reads Matthew 10:23 more toward resurrection and Matthew 16:27-28 toward the transfiguration. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=10076" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:56</a> | Would Ruth 4:18-22 count as evidence for a late Exodus date? | He says yes, if the genealogy is taken as a short line from the Exodus period down to David. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=10272" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:12</a> | Are there good reasons to radically revise ancient Near Eastern chronology? | No; he argues the current chronology rests on a very large body of evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=10460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:20</a> | Why are Matthew's and Luke's genealogies different, and does 1 Timothy 1:4 apply here? | He treats them as serving different purposes, one legal and one bloodline, and says 1 Timothy is not about Christ's pedigree. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=10775" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:35</a> | Does the word heaven have multiple meanings in the Bible? | Yes; it can mean sky, celestial realm, or the spiritual heaven depending on context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=10980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:00</a> | Have you ever considered creating an Ancient Egypt and the Bible board game? | No; he has thought more about video games and says board games are expensive to produce and market. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:01</a> | What are some DM tips after running a first D&D one-shot? | Plan ahead, expect players to miss things, and prepare surprises or complications in advance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:12:00</a> | How do you get into D&D, and what do you need to play? | Learn the rules, get dice or electronic dice, and treat it as collaborative roleplaying rather than a board game. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:12</a> | Do you make your own D&D character for the game? | Yes, but within the campaign limits the host sets for class, alignment, and level. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:48</a> | If someone is studying to be a worship leader, can they play a bard in the D&D game? | Maybe; he likes the idea and says he may open the game to clerics and bards. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11872" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:52</a> | Does Ahura Mazda count as pagan for the no-pagan-deities rule? | Yes; he says it does and that he would not treat Zoroastrian gods as valid for that rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=11963" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:23</a> | Was Genesis 49:8-10 originally aimed at Moses's time, or does it come from the monarchy? | He resists a monarchy-only reading because the Shiloh or messianic element reaches beyond that setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=12203" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:23</a> | Which tribe was Samson from? | Dan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=12420" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:00</a> | In 2 Corinthians 12, is the third heaven the spiritual heaven, or one layer among several heavens? | He says the text does not settle it clearly, but it may point to a higher level within a more complex heavenly realm. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=12790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:33:10</a> | Why do the genealogies in Matthew and Luke differ? | He argues Christ inherits through two lines, one legal and one genealogical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=13241" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:41</a> | Will a Numbers book be next after the plagues book? | Probably not; he says he wants to do a synchronisms book first, while another science-fiction draft is also in progress. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=13489" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:49</a> | Which fonts have you used in your books so far? | He says he uses Palatino Linotype in the novels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=13690" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:48:10</a> | What is the best font for comics? | He prefers a brush font unless someone is hand-lettering. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=13802" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:50:02</a> | Which fonts does David Falk recommend for PowerPoint? | He recommends Frutiger because it is clear at large and small sizes and also works well for maps and video text. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14016" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:36</a> | Has David ever made a comic book? | Yes; he drew an unpublished comic and started another, but says comic art is very slow to produce. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:21</a> | If you had to learn a language from the Indian subcontinent, which would you pick and why? | He would pick Sanskrit, partly because of its appearance on some Japanese swords. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14557" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:37</a> | Why do some Japanese swords have Sanskrit on them? | He links it to Buddhist influence on Japanese sword culture, with some inscriptions also serving decorative or practical uses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14656" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:16</a> | Why did Christianity become a minority in Japan? | Because it was outlawed and violently persecuted after an earlier period of strong growth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:21</a> | Is Christianity too pacifist for samurai? | He says no; at that time Christianity did not require samurai to stop being samurai, and divine lordship could appeal to them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=14859" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:39</a> | Do people make modern recreations of Christian Japanese sword fittings? | Not really for the scarce tsuba he wants; he says some modern Christian swords exist, but they look fake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qYRbD6EriCg?t=15048" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:10:48</a> | Are Japanese sword shows fun and extremely expensive? | He says they are fun and not necessarily expensive, since entry was cheap and small accessories let budget collectors participate. |
